segments.text: Information flow in a cell
X

a deoxyribonucleotide triphosphate ( dNTP )
(note similarity to ATP)
RNA polymerase enzymes do this too

5’ end
3’ end
RNA – single strand

strands are antiparallel
can only nucleotide to 3’ end
of growing strand

Promoter = specific DNA sequence
where RNA pol starts transcription

3’
5 ’
RNA does NOT stay base-paired to template DNA
DNA strands re-form base-pairs

Terminator = stop site
Promoter = start site

Eukaryotes ONLY
Modifications to mRNAs after transcription
1) at 5’ end – add modified G nucleotide “cap”
2) at 3’ end – add many A nucleotides
“poly-A tail”
3) splice out introns, join together exons

in eukaryotes
in prokaryotes – everything happens in the cytoplasm
no mRNA processing

Transcription
making RNA copy of DNA (gene )
Translation
making a protein using
mRNA as a template

Translation involves 3 classes of RNA
mRNA = messenger RNA
rRNA = ribosomal RNA
tRNA = transfer RNA
small subunit
large subunit
ribosome

each set of 3 bases =
one “ codon ”

DON’T MEMORIZE THIS!!
5’ CAU 3’ = histidine
First codon always = 5’ AUG 3 ’
3 stop codons

tRNA
transporter for amino acids
amino acid attached to 3’ end
anticodon
different tRNA for every codon

Ser
amino acid attached to
3’ end of tRNA
anticodon in tRNA base-pairs
with codon in mRNA
serine codon
tRNAs serve as “decoders”
of codons in mRNA

break this bond
Protein synthesis

ribosome moves along mRNA
until it gets to the stop codon

in prokaryotes:
everything in cytoplasm
no RNA processing
limited protein processing
